$comment: Time budget per topic = READING seat time only. Quizzes do NOT count toward delivery time — they are question-bank feedstock pooled into module/course-level assessments, not per-topic seat time. Weekly target = 90 min of reading across all topics in a week. Reading WPM = 200 (lower-end college reader). Hands-on artifact is NOT produced in this phase.

atomic.description: Tightly scoped topic (e.g. '6.2 Scalars — a single number representing one property'). Concept is foundational but small; padding the reading hurts quality.

quiz.$comment: Quiz time excluded from delivery budget — question-bank feedstock only.

diagram.override_when: topic introduces a visual primitive that benefits from a single hand-drawn glyph (rare for atomic topics)

standard.description: Mid-depth concept that needs context, an example, and a counter-example (e.g. '1.4 Why AI gives different answers to the same question').

quiz.$comment: Quiz time excluded from delivery budget — question-bank feedstock only.

deep.description: System-level or multi-part concept that needs a worked example end-to-end (e.g. '4.3 RAG — query → embed → retrieve → inject → respond').

quiz.$comment: Quiz time excluded from delivery budget — question-bank feedstock only.

diagram.intent: architecture or pipeline diagram showing the full flow

delivery_minutes_formula.$comment: expected_delivery_minutes = READING minutes only, written into topic.manifest.json. Quizzes are NOT seat time (question-bank feedstock), so quiz_minutes is no longer summed in.

weekly_balance_rule.$comment: course-drift critic enforces sum(topic.expected_delivery_minutes) per week. Soft warn at ±15%; fail at ±25%.

classification_signals.$comment: Heuristics the corpus-builder uses to pick a profile before HITL confirmation.
atomic_signals: title is a single noun or compound noun under 8 words
Core Concepts section is one tight paragraph
topic ID maps to a single line in the xlsx source
deep_signals: topic appears in a week with a 'Lab' Lab/Activity

hitl_confirmation.$comment: After the corpus passes its critic, the orchestrator computes a WEEK-AWARE recommended depth profile (intrinsic topic complexity + remaining budget for the week) and surfaces it via AskUserQuestion. The user either accepts the recommendation or overrides; there is no 'choose from scratch' path.
hitl_confirmation.question_template: Recommended: {profile} (~{minutes} min). Week running total would be {week_total}/90 min. Accept?
recommendation_inputs: sum of expected_delivery_minutes for already-approved topics in this week
count of topics in the week still to be processed
